Get Zero OS Fast

Clone it, run one setup command, and open the Windows-like shell UI. Zero OS is designed to be easy for GitHub users to copy, inspect, and run locally.

git clone https://github.com/Viomnz/Zero-OS.git cd Zero-OS .\zero_os_launcher.ps1 first-run Start-Process ".\zero_os_shell.html"
Open Repository Open Releases Open Shell UI
Open source Shell UI included GitHub intake built in Local-only runtime
1. Copy
Copy the clone command or download a release zip.
2. Run
Run .\zero_os_launcher.ps1 first-run.
3. Open
Open zero_os_shell.html and use the built-in UI.

Share Options

  • Clone with Git
  • Download repo zip
  • Use local export bundle command
  • Use dedicated Zero AI bundle commands
  • Open zero_os_shell.html after setup
python src/main.py "zero os export bundle" python src/main.py "zero os share package" python src/main.py "zero ai export bundle" python src/main.py "zero ai share package"

Get Zero OS

  • Clone from GitHub
  • Download the repository zip for a no-Git copy
  • Run .\zero_os_launcher.ps1 first-run
  • Open zero_os_shell.html

Get Zero AI

  • Export the Zero AI source bundle only
  • Create a dedicated Zero AI zip for GitHub users
  • Share the generated dist/zero_ai_bundle_*.zip artifact
python src/main.py "zero ai export bundle" python src/main.py "zero ai share package"

Status

GitHub Pages static mode active
  • No local runtime fetch dependencies
  • Safe public overview page
  • Full dashboard remains local-only

Project Links

  • README
  • ROADMAP
  • Windows-like Shell UI
  • Local Dashboard File
  • Repository

First Run

  • Run .\zero_os_launcher.ps1 first-run
  • Open zero_os_shell.html
  • Verify with python src/main.py \"resilience status\"
  • Optional: .\zero_os_launcher.ps1 start-shell-bridge

Architecture Focus

  • AI Runtime Layers: reliability, memory update, adaptation, evolution
  • Security: guard baseline, quarantine, signed policy flows
  • Kernel R&D: boot trust, memory manager, scheduler, syscall ABI

For full runtime view, open zero_os_dashboard.html locally. For a clean shareable local copy, use python src/main.py "zero os export bundle".
